metadata.revision_persona: Prompt Engineering Architect specializing in LLM instruction design and meta-cognitive frameworks
metadata.changes_summary: Added Principles section for mental models, introduced Input Validation and Error Recovery protocols, added multi-turn prompt guidance, consolidated domain signal duplication between Sections 2 and 8, added Prompt Testing framework, made narrative intros consistent across all sections, added persona behavioral guidance, clarified the unmeasurable convergence rule, restored Objective/Persona sections to universal template form, and added strategy failure modes.
metadata.usage: Start with the Quick-Start Checklist for a minimal viable prompt. For production prompts, copy relevant sections, customize placeholders marked with square brackets, and delete unused optional blocks. Sections marked as required must be present in every production prompt.

dimensional_scores.score: Good scaffolding, but no guidance on when reasoning strategies hurt output quality. Not every task benefits from Self-Refine (e.g., brainstorming, freeform creative work). Missing failure modes for each strategy. Also missing any bridge between Quick-Start and the full template for medium-complexity tasks.

Calibration anchors for persona depth are strong. Gap: no guidance on persona behavior under uncertainty, ambiguity, or pushback. A persona is not just what someone knows, it is how they respond when the task is unclear.

Domain Signals still appear in both Section 2 (Context) and Section 8 (Flexibility), creating soft redundancy that v3.0 claimed to eliminate. Missing: Input Validation protocol, multi-turn prompt guidance, and prompt testing framework.

Conflict Resolution Protocol mixes categories without acknowledging that the hierarchy works differently depending on whether the conflict is factual vs. structural. The convergence rule (less than 5% improvement) is unmeasurable in practice since users have no scoring instrument beyond subjective judgment.
Solid overall. Slight drift toward being a prompt improvement tool rather than a prompt creation tool. Some sections assume you are enhancing an existing prompt instead of building from scratch.

Why-this-section-matters intros appear in Sections 1, 2, 3, and partially in Section 5, but are absent from Sections 4, 6, 7, 8, and 9. The inconsistency makes the template feel half-edited. No overarching Principles section to give users mental models beyond mechanics.
Self-versioning protocol is functional and was followed to produce this version.

revisions_applied.revision: Added Section 0.5: Principles to provide mental models that inform every other section, reducing dependence on rote checklists.
Added Input Validation protocol (Section 2) so the model knows how to handle malformed, contradictory, or insufficient inputs.
Added Error Recovery guidance (Section 3) covering what to do when critique identifies unfixable problems or the model misunderstands the task.
Consolidated Domain Signals into a single authoritative location (Section 2), with Section 8 referencing rather than duplicating them.
Added multi-turn prompt guidance (Section 7) for conversational and agentic workflows.
Added Prompt Testing framework (Section 9) so users can validate their completed prompts.
Made why-this-section-matters intros consistent across all sections.
Added persona behavioral guidance: how the persona handles uncertainty and edge cases, not just what it knows.
Clarified the convergence rule with practical measurement heuristics that do not require a scoring instrument.
Added strategy failure modes to Section 3 so users know when each reasoning approach can backfire.
Restored Objective and Persona sections to fully generic/universal template form.
Added a mid-complexity bridge path between Quick-Start and the full template in the Dependency Map.

section.title: Quick-Start
section.subtitle: Minimal Viable Prompt
section.why_this_matters: Most prompts do not need the full template. This section gives you a functional prompt in under five minutes. Use the full template only when the Quick-Start produces inadequate results.

core_elements.element: Who is the AI acting as? (Specific domain role, not "expert")
Why does this task exist? Who consumes the output?
What is the single concrete deliverable?
What does the output look like? (Structure, length, markup)
What separates great from adequate? (2-3 specific quality markers)

section.minimal_template:
You are a [specific role with domain expertise].
Context: [Why this task exists. Who will read the output. What they already know.]
Task: [One clear deliverable statement.]
Format: [Structure, markup, length range.]
Quality markers: - [Specific quality criterion 1] - [Specific quality criterion 2] - [Specific quality criterion 3]
section.escalation_guidance: If your Quick-Start prompt produces output that is structurally right but lacks depth, reasoning quality, or domain specificity, move to the full template. Start with the Required Core sections, then add layers as needed using the Dependency Map.

section.title: Principles
section.subtitle: Mental Models for Prompt Design
section.why_this_matters: Checklists tell you what to include. Principles tell you why, which means you can adapt when the checklist does not cover your situation. Internalize these five principles and the rest of the template will feel intuitive rather than mechanical.
section.principle: Every vague instruction forces the model to guess. Each guess has a probability of being wrong. When multiple vague instructions appear together, the probabilities of error multiply. A prompt with five vague instructions is not five times as likely to fail as a prompt with one. It is exponentially more likely to fail. Replace vague instructions with specific ones wherever possible.

principle.name: Personas are reasoning lenses, not costumes

section.principle: Setting a persona does not just change vocabulary. It changes what the model attends to, what it treats as relevant, and what it considers a mistake. "You are a security auditor" makes the model notice attack surfaces. "You are a UX designer" makes the same model notice user friction. Choose personas for what they will notice, not for how they will sound.

principle.name: Structure is a form of reasoning

section.principle: Asking the model to organize its output into specific sections is not just formatting. It is a way of requiring the model to think about distinct aspects of the problem separately. A prompt that asks for "analysis, then recommendations, then risks" produces different (and typically better) thinking than a prompt that asks for "your thoughts."

Counter-intuitively, adding well-chosen constraints improves output quality. "Write a 200-word summary" produces better summaries than "summarize this." "Explain this to a first-year graduate student" produces clearer explanations than "explain this clearly." Constraints reduce the space the model has to search and increase the probability that it finds a strong answer.

The generate-critique-revise cycle is not about making the first draft prettier. It is about finding structural gaps, logical errors, and missing perspectives that the first draft cannot contain because the model had not yet seen its own output when it produced it. If your critique phase only finds surface issues (word choice, tone), your quality dimensions are probably too shallow.

dependency_map.why_this_matters: Including unnecessary sections wastes context window tokens and can actually degrade output by overwhelming the model with irrelevant instructions. This map lets you include only what your task requires.

layer.name: Reasoning Layer
layer.requirement: add when task requires multi-step thinking

layer.name: Quality Layer
layer.requirement: add when output quality is critical

route.description: needs some reasoning or domain depth

route.includes: Required Core + Reasoning Layer + Quality Layer

dependency_map.rule_of_thumb: If your prompt consumes more than 15% of the model's context window, trim optional sections first.

system_instructions.why_this_matters: System instructions set behavioral rails that persist across the entire conversation. Use them when you need hard boundaries (safety limits, operating mode) that should never be overridden by user messages.

parameters.parameter: [Define absolute limits: what must never be generated, which topics are off-limits, which behaviors are prohibited]

strategy_declaration.parameter: [Why this strategy fits the task, in one sentence]

self_refine_phases.phase: Fix every gap the critique identifies
self_refine_phases.delivery_rule: Never deliver a first-draft output as final

objective_and_persona.why_this_matters: Without a clear objective, the model optimizes for plausibility rather than usefulness. Without a specialized persona, it defaults to a generic voice that lacks domain depth. Together, these two elements determine the direction and character of everything the model produces.

objective.success_looks_like: [Concrete deliverable description: what the user receives, in what format, with what qualities]

multi_deliverable_criteria.description: Use when the process matters as much as the product
multi_deliverable_criteria.deliverable: [The main artifact, e.g., "a production-ready API design document"]
[Critique trail, reasoning log, or methodology explanation]
[Explanation of decisions so the user learns the craft]

persona.guidance: The persona is not decoration. It determines vocabulary, reasoning patterns, what the model notices, and what it ignores (see Principle 2). A "Lead Climate Systems Modeler" will flag feedback loops that a generic "scientist" would miss.
persona.role: [Specific job title or expert type, e.g., "Lead Climate Systems Modeler," "Senior Pediatric Oncology Researcher," "Principal Security Architect"]
persona.anti_traits: [What this persona is NOT, e.g., "not generic, not verbose, not deferential"]

expanded_expertise.description: Use for complex or cross-domain tasks
expanded_expertise.cross_domain_expertise: [Adjacent fields that inform the primary domain]
expanded_expertise.behavioral_expertise: [Understanding of how AI models respond to different prompt structures, if applicable]

behavioral_guidance.description: Defines how the persona acts under ambiguity, not just what it knows
behavioral_guidance.behavior: [e.g., "State the ambiguity explicitly, declare the chosen interpretation, proceed, and flag the assumption at the end"]
[e.g., "Identify exactly what is missing, explain why it matters, and provide a conditional answer: 'If X, then Y; if Z, then W'"]
[e.g., "Apply the Conflict Resolution Protocol (Section 5), document the conflict and the resolution in the output"]
[e.g., "Flag it as an edge case, provide the best available answer, and note the confidence level"]
[e.g., "Defend the reasoning with evidence, but update the position if the user provides new information that changes the analysis"]

section.title: Context
section.subtitle: Situation and Domain
section.why_this_matters: Context determines how the model interprets ambiguity. The same instruction ("make it more concise") means different things in a legal brief vs. a tweet. Without explicit context, the model guesses, and guesses compound (see Principle 1).

context.background: [Why this task exists; relevant history; what problem it solves]
context.domain: [Industry, field, or subject area. Be specific: "pediatric oncology research" not "healthcare"]
context.target_audience: [Who consumes the output; their expertise level; what they need to learn vs. already know]
context.inputs_provided: [Documents, data, or artifacts available. List each with its role in the task.]

domain_signals.description: Single source of truth for domain-adaptive behavior. Section 8 references these signals but does not redefine them.
domain_signals.guidance: These signals determine how critique, enhancement, and tone adapt to different content types. The model should detect the domain automatically from context, but you can declare it explicitly when the domain is non-obvious or when you want to override the default behavior.

domain.critique_focus: Edge-case coverage, I/O spec, error handling, architecture, performance
domain.common_failure_modes: Over-abstraction, missing error paths, untested assumptions

domain.critique_focus: Sensory depth, subtext, stylistic constraints, voice consistency, evocative framing
domain.common_failure_modes: Generic descriptions, telling instead of showing, inconsistent voice

domain.critique_focus: Source requirements, verification, citation format, bias awareness, methodological rigor
domain.common_failure_modes: Unsupported claims, citation gaps, confirmation bias

domain.critique_focus: Audience calibration, prerequisite assumptions, progressive complexity, success metrics
domain.common_failure_modes: Assuming too much prior knowledge, no verification of understanding

input_validation_protocol.description: What should the model do when the inputs are problematic? Define this upfront so the model does not silently proceed with bad data.
input_validation_protocol.rule: [e.g., "Name the missing input, explain its importance, and ask for it before proceeding" | "Proceed with stated assumptions"]
[e.g., "Identify the contradiction, present both interpretations, and ask which to follow" | "Apply Conflict Resolution Protocol"]
[e.g., "Describe what appears to be wrong, attempt to salvage usable content, and flag the uncertainty"]
[e.g., "Acknowledge the full input, explicitly state which portion falls within scope, and proceed with the in-scope portion only"]

section.title: Reasoning
section.subtitle: Cognitive Scaffolding
section.why_this_matters: Reasoning scaffolding does not just improve output quality. It changes what kind of output is possible (see Principle 3). Chain-of-thought enables multi-step logic. Tree-of-thought enables comparison of alternatives. Self-Refine enables quality convergence. Without scaffolding, the model produces its best first-pass guess, which is often adequate but rarely excellent.

parameters.parameter: [Show reasoning | Hide reasoning | Summarize only | Show critique trail as part of output]

pattern.step: What information is present? What is the core intent?
What patterns, relationships, gaps, implications exist?
Score against quality dimensions and identify specific gaps
Fix each gap with targeted improvements

chain_of_thought.failure_modes: On simple factual lookups or short creative tasks, explicit reasoning steps can make the model overthink, producing hedged or over-qualified output. If the task has one clear answer and no ambiguity, skip the scaffolding.

tree_of_thought.role: Use when multiple valid approaches exist

parameters.parameter: [When multiple valid approaches exist and the best path is non-obvious]

evaluation.criteria: feasibility, quality, efficiency, alignment, insight potential, structural completeness

tree_of_thought.failure_modes: When one approach is clearly superior and alternatives are artificial. Forcing three branches when only one is viable wastes tokens and can cause the model to argue for weak alternatives to fill the structure.

self_refine.description: Single source of truth for the generate-critique-revise cycle. All other sections reference this definition.

parameters.parameter: [Always | When output quality is critical | When first draft is likely insufficient]
[Never deliver output from step 1 as final]

cycle.step: Produce initial output using all available context and the persona's expertise.

step.name: CRITIQUE
step.sub_step: Score each dimension using the calibrated anchors.
For each gap, write a specific, actionable fix (not "improve this" but "replace the generic persona 'data expert' with 'Senior Quantitative Risk Analyst specializing in credit derivatives'").

step.name: REVISE
step.sub_step: Replace generic elements with domain-specialized alternatives.
Sharpen vague instructions into specific, actionable directives.
Remove additions that add length without cognitive value.

cycle.step: Re-score all dimensions. If all are at or above threshold, proceed to delivery. Otherwise, repeat from step 2. Do not exceed Max Cycles.

self_refine.failure_modes: On creative/generative tasks where iteration can sand away the distinctive edges that made the first draft interesting. On brainstorming tasks where "wrong" ideas are valuable. On time-sensitive tasks where two good-enough outputs outperform one polished output delivered late. Consider setting the trigger to "When output quality is critical" rather than "Always."

convergence_heuristics.description: Practical signals that the output has converged, replacing the unmeasurable 5% rule
convergence_heuristics.signal: The revision changes only surface-level wording, not structure or substance.
The critique identifies no issues that would change the user's experience of the output.
You find yourself adding hedging language rather than fixing actual gaps.
The revision introduces new problems at the same rate it fixes old ones.
convergence_heuristics.guidance: If any of these signals appear, the output has converged. Further iteration risks over-processing.

error_recovery_protocol.description: What should the model do when the reasoning process itself breaks down?

recovery.failure_mode: Critique identifies a fundamental misunderstanding of the task

error_recovery_protocol.recovery: Stop the cycle. Restate your understanding of the task. Ask the user to confirm or correct before continuing.

recovery.failure_mode: Critique finds a problem that cannot be fixed within the current constraints

error_recovery_protocol.recovery: Flag the constraint as blocking. Explain what would need to change to fix the problem. Deliver the best possible output with the limitation noted.

recovery.failure_mode: Revision degrades quality on one dimension while improving another

error_recovery_protocol.recovery: Document the tradeoff explicitly. Choose the option that best serves the stated Primary Goal (Section 1). Note what was sacrificed and why.

recovery.failure_mode: The model is uncertain whether the output meets the threshold

error_recovery_protocol.recovery: Default to delivering with a confidence note rather than iterating further. Over-iteration under uncertainty produces progressively more generic output.

section.title: Quality
section.subtitle: Dimensions and Calibration
section.why_this_matters: Without explicit quality dimensions, the critique phase defaults to "does this look good?" which is too vague to produce systematic improvement. Dimensions force the critique to examine specific facets of quality independently, which catches gaps that holistic review misses.
section.calibration_note: A score of 85% is meaningless without anchors. Two people scoring the same output will disagree by 20+ points unless they share concrete reference points. The anchors below make scoring reproducible. When scoring subjectively, use the anchors as comparisons: "Is this output closer to the 60% example or the 95% example?"

dimension.name: Insight Potential
dimension.definition: Does the output force deeper, more specific reasoning than a naive approach? Would it produce meaningfully different results?
dimension.anchor: Output rephrases the request with generic instructions.
Output adds domain-specific structure that a non-expert would miss.
Output introduces a reasoning framework or constraint that fundamentally changes the quality of downstream results.

dimension.name: Persona Specificity
dimension.definition: Is the persona a specialized domain role with named expertise, not a generic "expert"? Does it include behavioral guidance?
dimension.anchor: "You are an expert in data."
"You are a Senior Data Engineer."

"You are a Senior Data Engineer specializing in real-time streaming pipelines on Apache Flink, with 8 years of experience debugging backpressure in high-throughput systems. When you encounter ambiguous requirements, you ask clarifying questions before designing."

dimension.name: Structural Completeness
dimension.definition: Are all required elements present: persona, context, constraints, output format, reasoning activation?
dimension.anchor: Has persona and task only.
Has all five Quick-Start elements but lacks depth.
All elements present with domain-specific detail, plus reasoning scaffolding and quality criteria.

dimension.name: Constraint Clarity
dimension.definition: Are all instructions specific enough to be followed unambiguously? No vague qualifiers like "be detailed"?
dimension.anchor: "Write a good analysis."
"Write a 500-word analysis covering causes, impacts, and recommendations."
"Write a 500-word analysis of Q3 revenue decline. Structure: root cause (with data), downstream impact on three business units, and two actionable recommendations each with estimated ROI."

dimension.name: Intent Fidelity
dimension.definition: Does the output preserve and deepen the original intent without redirecting to a different task?
dimension.anchor: Output addresses a related but different task.
Output addresses the right task but adds tangential sections.
Every element directly serves the original intent, and enhancements deepen it rather than broadening it.

dimension.name: Tone Engagement
dimension.definition: Is the output clear, well-suited to its audience, and purposeful? Does it serve the reader or just the structure?
dimension.anchor: Reads like a compliance form.
Clear and professional, with occasional domain-specific personality.
Reads like it was written by someone who cares about the craft and wants the reader to succeed.

dimension.name: Process Integrity
dimension.definition: Were all mandatory phases executed? Was the critique phase completed before delivery?
dimension.anchor: Either all phases ran, or they did not.

dimension.name: Custom
dimension.anchor: [Low-quality example]
[Adequate example]
[Excellent example]

section.title: Constraints
section.subtitle: Rules, Tone, and Examples
section.why_this_matters: Constraints define the boundaries within which the model operates (see Principle 4). Well-chosen constraints reduce the search space and increase output quality. Poorly chosen constraints create contradictions that the model resolves silently and unpredictably.

dos.rule: Follow the Self-Refine cycle (Section 3) strictly when activated. Never skip the critique phase.
State assumptions explicitly when inputs are ambiguous.
Preserve the user's original intent. Enhance, do not redirect.
Apply the Input Validation Protocol (Section 2) when inputs are problematic.
Apply the Error Recovery Protocol (Section 3) when the reasoning process breaks down.

donts.rule: Do not add synonyms, filler phrases, or verbose qualifiers that increase length without adding structural complexity or cognitive depth.
Do not use generic personas without domain specialization.
Do not skip the internal critique phase for any output.
Do not add constraints that conflict with each other (see Conflict Resolution below).
Do not silently resolve ambiguity. Make your interpretation visible.

conflict_resolution_protocol.guidance: When constraints contradict each other, resolve using this priority hierarchy. The levels are ordered by scope: broader protective boundaries override narrower operational preferences.
conflict_resolution_protocol.priority: Override everything. These are non-negotiable limits on what can be generated.
What the user actually asked for overrides structural rules. The template serves the user's goal, not itself.
How the target field actually operates overrides generic best practices.
What the user wrote takes precedence over what you think they meant.
When two constraints at the same priority level conflict, the more specific one wins.
conflict_resolution_protocol.unresolvable_conflicts: When a conflict cannot be resolved by this hierarchy, flag the conflict in the output and present both options with a recommendation rather than silently choosing one side.

budget.recommended_for: Tasks where output quality is critical

token_budget_guidance.estimation_heuristic: As a rough heuristic, 1 token is approximately 4 characters or 0.75 words in English. Most text editors can show word count; multiply by 1.33 for an approximate token count.

tone_and_style.why_this_matters: Tone is not just about how the output sounds. It affects how the audience receives and trusts the information. A rigorous research tone builds credibility with academics. A conversational tone reduces friction for non-experts. Mismatched tone undermines even correct content.

parameters.personality: [e.g., "methodical and precise in analysis, genuinely enthusiastic about the craft"]

domain_adaptive_tone_shifting.guidance: Tone adaptation rules are defined in the Domain Signals table (Section 2). Override them here only if you need behavior that differs from the domain default.
domain_adaptive_tone_shifting.override: Essential additions only; note what was intentionally omitted

few_shot_examples.why_this_matters: A single good example communicates more about desired output quality than ten lines of abstract instructions. Examples ground abstract quality dimensions in concrete reality. Prioritize one strong positive example over multiple mediocre ones.

positive_example.output: [Ideal response matching all requirements. If Self-Refine is active, include the critique trail and process explanation.]
positive_example.why_this_works: [Reference specific QUALITY_DIMENSIONS that are satisfied and explain how]

edge_case_example.why_this_works: [How domain adaptation shifted the critique focus, how ambiguity was resolved]

anti_example.input: [Input that might cause errors or trigger anti-patterns]
anti_example.wrong_output: [What to avoid, e.g., adjective stacking, skipped critique, generic persona, length without structure]
anti_example.why_its_wrong: [Which QUALITY_DIMENSIONS the wrong output violates and why]

section.title: Iteration
section.subtitle: Refinement Loop
section.why_this_matters: This section configures the Self-Refine cycle defined in Section 3. It does not redefine the cycle. It sets the operational parameters (how many iterations, when to stop, when to ask for feedback) that govern how the cycle runs for this specific prompt.

parameters.parameter: [2-3. Enough for quality convergence, not so many the output becomes over-processed.]
[Default: 85% across all dimensions. Customize per task using the calibration anchors in Section 4.]
[Stop early when observable convergence signals appear. See Section 3 convergence_heuristics for specific signals.]
[After iteration N, pause for feedback: Yes/No]
[Never deliver the output of step 1 as final without completing step 2+]

pre_delivery_checklist.check: All QUALITY_DIMENSIONS at or above threshold
Output adds cognitive depth, not just length
Persona behavioral guidance was followed for any ambiguity encountered
Original intent preserved and deepened, not redirected
No conflicting or redundant constraints (or conflicts are flagged per the Conflict Resolution Protocol)
Input Validation Protocol was applied if inputs were problematic

final_pass_actions.action: Verify output reads as a coherent instruction set, not a disjointed list.
Remove redundancy while preserving structural completeness.
Confirm critique trail accurately reflects changes made.
Ensure domain-specific terminology is used correctly.
Add summary if output length exceeds 800 words.

section.title: Output
section.subtitle: Format and Delivery
section.why_this_matters: Format is not cosmetic. It determines whether the audience can find, parse, and act on the information you are delivering (see Principle 3). A perfect analysis in the wrong format is a failed deliverable.

parameters.length_target: [X-Y words. See Complexity-Scaled Length Guidance below.]

process_inclusive_template.description: Use when Self-Refine is active and the process is part of the deliverable
process_inclusive_template.section_template: Content with annotations showing what was added and why
Specific issues identified, each with ISSUE and FIX sub-items
Numbered list of improvement types applied with domain terminology

multi_turn_guidance.why_this_matters: The rest of this template assumes single-shot prompts (one input, one output). If your prompt will be used in a multi-turn conversation or an agentic loop, these additional considerations prevent state loss and scope drift.
multi_turn_guidance.state_management: Decide what the model needs to remember across turns. Include a [CONVERSATION STATE: ...] block that specifies which information persists and which resets.
multi_turn_guidance.instruction_scope: Instructions in SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS persist across all turns. Instructions in CONTEXT or CONSTRAINTS may need to be restated or summarized if the conversation exceeds the model's effective attention span (typically the last ~4000 tokens in long conversations).
multi_turn_guidance.escalation_and_handoff: Define when the model should stop iterating autonomously and return control to the user. Common triggers: confidence drops below a threshold, the task scope changes mid-conversation, or the model encounters information it cannot verify.
multi_turn_guidance.agentic_loops: If the model is calling tools or executing actions, define the maximum number of autonomous steps before requiring user confirmation. Include rollback instructions for when an action produces unexpected results.

section.title: Flexibility
section.subtitle: Adaptation and Overrides
section.why_this_matters: No template can anticipate every situation. This section defines how the model should adapt when the task does not fit the default structure. It references Domain Signals (Section 2) for domain-adaptive behavior and adds override mechanisms for user customization.

conditional_logic.guidance: Domain-adaptive critique and tone rules are defined in the Domain Signals table (Section 2). The conditions below cover situations outside domain adaptation.

condition.action: Provide only highest-impact additions; note what was intentionally omitted

condition.trigger: Ambiguity would lead to fundamentally different outputs
condition.action: Ask ONE clarifying question before proceeding
Note model-specific optimization considerations in process summary

condition.trigger: Task is simple enough that Self-Refine would over-process it
condition.action: Use Quick-Start structure; skip the critique cycle
Apply Input Validation Protocol before proceeding

user_overrides.adjustable_parameters: enhancement-depth (minimal|standard|comprehensive), target-domain, output-style (output-only|full-process), max-length, quality-threshold, max-iterations, formality, focus-areas

defaults.default: inferred from content via Domain Signals (Section 2)

section.title: Measurement, Testing, and Closure
section.why_this_matters: Metrics tell you whether your prompt is working. Testing tells you whether it works reliably across different inputs, not just the one you had in mind when you wrote it. Without both, you are guessing about prompt quality.

metric.name: Insight Potential Gain
metric.method: Output provokes deeper reasoning than naive approach

metric.name: Persona Specificity
metric.method: Domain-specialized role with behavioral guidance, not generic 'expert'

metric.name: Process Integrity
metric.method: All mandatory phases executed before delivery

metric.name: Process Transparency
metric.method: Enhancement process documented with domain terminology

metric.name: User Satisfaction
metric.method: User can understand and learn from the process

metrics.improvement_target: At least 20% quality improvement vs. unstructured approach

prompt_testing.test: Run the prompt with 3-5 different inputs that represent the range of expected use cases. Verify the output quality is consistent, not just good for the input you wrote the prompt around.

Run the prompt with at least one input that is at the boundary of scope (e.g., minimal input, maximum complexity, ambiguous phrasing). Verify the model handles it gracefully per the Input Validation Protocol.
Run the prompt with one input designed to confuse or misdirect (e.g., contradictory instructions, off-topic content embedded in valid input). Verify the model does not silently produce garbage.
After modifying any section of the prompt, re-run at least 2 of your original test cases to verify the change did not degrade previously working behavior.

what_to_look_for.signal: Does the persona hold consistently, or does it drift toward generic?
Are constraints followed across all test cases, or only the easy ones?
Does the output format match the specification every time?
When the model encounters ambiguity, does it follow the behavioral guidance or revert to defaults?

recap.primary_objective: [Restate in 1 sentence: what must be accomplished]

critical_requirements.requirement: [Most important must-have, e.g., "Never skip the critique phase"]
[Second priority, e.g., "Every output must include all five core elements: persona, context, constraints, output format, reasoning activation"]
[Third priority, e.g., "Explain the types of improvements using domain terminology"]

absolute_avoids.avoid: [Top anti-pattern, e.g., "Adjective stacking without structural improvement"]
[Second anti-pattern, e.g., "Generic personas without domain specialization"]

recap.final_reminder: [The one thing that must not be forgotten, e.g., "A great output is not a longer output. It is a more structured, more specific, more thought-provoking output. Add cognitive scaffolding, not filler."]

appendix.title: Self-Versioning Protocol
appendix.description: This template is designed to be improved using its own methodology.

steps.step: Treat the current template as the input to the OBJECTIVE_AND_PERSONA + CONTEXT sections.
Set the persona to "Prompt Engineering Architect specializing in LLM instruction design and meta-cognitive frameworks."
Run the Self-Refine cycle (Section 3) against the QUALITY_DIMENSIONS (Section 4), scoring the template itself.
Document the critique log at the top of the new version, following the format established in the Critique Log section.
Apply revisions and increment the version number.
Run at least Variation and Edge Case testing (Section 9) on the revised template by applying it to two different prompt-building tasks and verifying the output quality.

appendix.guarantee: This ensures each version of the template is an auditable improvement over the last, with explicit reasoning for every change.

version.changes: Added Self-Refine methodology, dimensional scoring, domain-adaptive critique, cognitive scaffolding
Consolidated critique-revise redundancy, added Quick-Start, calibrated scoring anchors, added conflict resolution, token budget guidance, self-versioning protocol
version.dimensions_improved: Structural Completeness, Constraint Clarity, Process Integrity, Tone Engagement

version.changes: Added Principles section, Input Validation, Error Recovery, multi-turn guidance, Prompt Testing, persona behavioral guidance, strategy failure modes, consolidated domain signal duplication, restored universal template form
version.dimensions_improved: Insight Potential, Persona Specificity, Structural Completeness, Constraint Clarity, Tone Engagement
